acceptance_targets: [ch14_award_01] Pass if, after the diagnosis exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:235-247, the revised ending preserves Doug's homecoming and the two-weeks-out taping threat but states the scripted-versus-accidental insight no more than once more. Fail if the closing pages still contain multiple separate sentences/paragraphs that all restate that planned material dies and accidental truth works.

[ch14_craft_01] Pass if a cold reader can quote at least two concrete lines Doug actually says in the Detroit run at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:195-205, can identify one observable audience-response change before snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:207, and no longer has to rely on three consecutive 'He talked about...' summaries to understand why the room finally listens. Fail if the beat remains mostly paraphrase.

[ch14_craft_02] Pass if a reader can trace the revised trip at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:133 without any impossible direction reversal: Detroit is not south of Toledo, and the outbound route no longer conflicts with the later I-75 south return at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:213. Fail if the geography still asks the reader to ignore a map.

[ch14_dialogue_01] Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:235-237 no longer contains the exact phrase 'The material doesn't work' or a close echo of the full calibration sentence, and Doug's realization is phrased in language that reads as his own. Fail if the revised exchange still reproduces the calibration wording recognizably.

[F05] Re-read lines 3422-3688. Count negation-correction pairs: pass if 7 or fewer. The driving reflection at lines 3636-3683 must be at least 15 lines shorter than current. The depreciation metaphor at line 3683 must end at 'structural, not cosmetic' — the extension must be cut. The 'not laughter, but attention' formulation at line 3624 must be preserved.

[F15] Re-read line 3656 and surrounding context. The phrase 'The material doesn't work' must not appear. Doug's replacement must name the same failure using insurance-adjuster or Doug-specific vocabulary that shows he has processed the three performances into his own diagnosis.

[F19] Re-read the full revised Chapter 14. The chapter must contain no more than two fully rendered performance scenes. If Cleveland is preserved, it must be 15 lines or fewer. The post-show processing must use at least two different formats across the remaining cities (not every one can end with a Simone phone call following the 'How was it? / Bad. / Bad how?' template). The total chapter length should be at least 50 lines shorter than current. The Detroit unscripted 8-minute segment must be preserved in full.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, after the diagnosis exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:235-247, the revised ending preserves Doug's homecoming and the two-weeks-out taping threat but states the scripted-versus-accidental insight no more than once more. Fail if the closing pages still contain multiple separate sentences/paragraphs that all restate that planned material dies and accidental truth works.
findings.problem: The chapter's state shift lands by the Detroit call, then the ending keeps re-proving the same thesis in fresh wording. After Doug says the material fails, the narration reiterates the same discovery four more times instead of converting it into a sharper final turn, so the last pages feel longer than the dramatic payload they add.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress and re-sequence the ending at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:239-265. Keep the must-land beats that matter: Simone confirming the diagnosis, Doug arriving home, and the taping now looming two weeks away. Cut or recast the extra thesis restatements at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:249, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:261, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:263, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:265 so the chapter closes on one inference plus one physical end image rather than repeated paraphrase.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can quote at least two concrete lines Doug actually says in the Detroit run at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:195-205, can identify one observable audience-response change before snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:207, and no longer has to rely on three consecutive 'He talked about...' summaries to understand why the room finally listens. Fail if the beat remains mostly paraphrase.
findings.problem: The chapter's decisive Detroit breakthrough is handled as narrated summary instead of live scene. At the exact moment Doug accidentally finds something real, the prose retreats into 'He talked about...' paraphrase and thematic explanation. In a comedy-driven chapter, this is the comic equivalent of sanitization drift: the narrator explains the set instead of letting the reader hear the set and feel the room turn.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:195-205 as an enacted performance beat rather than reported summary. Put at least several actual lines from Doug on the page about the drive, the hotels, or the rest stops; show the heckler/date/audience response shifting in real time; keep the later failure at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:207-209, but earn it through rendered scene rather than retrospective explanation.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a reader can trace the revised trip at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:133 without any impossible direction reversal: Detroit is not south of Toledo, and the outbound route no longer conflicts with the later I-75 south return at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:213. Fail if the geography still asks the reader to ignore a map.
findings.problem: The Pittsburgh-to-Detroit travel description contains a continuity-breaking geography error. The route goes 'through Pennsylvania and across the narrow top of West Virginia and up through Ohio to Toledo and then south to the city,' but Detroit is north of Toledo, and this routing does not track with the later southbound return on I-75. That is a concrete spatial impossibility, not a style issue.
findings.rewrite_direction: Correct the route at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:133 so the drive follows a plausible Pittsburgh-to-Detroit path and the cardinal directions are accurate. If Toledo remains in the sentence, Detroit must be reached north of it, not south of it; if West Virginia remains, the route logic must still read as physically coherent on the page.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:235-237 no longer contains the exact phrase 'The material doesn't work' or a close echo of the full calibration sentence, and Doug's realization is phrased in language that reads as his own. Fail if the revised exchange still reproduces the calibration wording recognizably.
findings.problem: The line 'The material doesn't work' echoes a calibration-only example line from the style bible ('The material doesn't work. You know that.'). The review rules are explicit that example lines must not appear verbatim or near-verbatim in draft text. This is a direct dialogue-calibration violation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the phrasing at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:235-237 with a fresh, character-true articulation of Doug's realization. Keep the factual despair, but do not reuse the style-bible wording or a close paraphrase of it.

findings.acceptance_test: Re-read lines 3422-3688. Count negation-correction pairs: pass if 7 or fewer. The driving reflection at lines 3636-3683 must be at least 15 lines shorter than current. The depreciation metaphor at line 3683 must end at 'structural, not cosmetic' — the extension must be cut. The 'not laughter, but attention' formulation at line 3624 must be preserved.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3450, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3504, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3524, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3528, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3624, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3630, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3636, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3683
findings.problem: Prose formula saturation. Chapter 14 (the three-city tour) is the second-longest chapter and accumulates formulaic constructions across three performance venues and three post-show reflections. The negation-correction pattern appears in nearly every performance description: 'Not absent — wrong' (3450), 'not laughter exactly, but attention' (3528/3624), 'not a gap so much as a different country' (3582). The post-show driving scenes repeat the 'he thought about X. He thought about Y.' inventory pattern (3636-3683) — listing what Doug is processing instead of showing it through detail. The 'which was' appositive appears ~11 times. The 'the way' comparison appears ~8 times. KEEP the negation-correction at line 3624 ('not laughter, but the attention you give to something true') — it captures the chapter's strongest moment. REPLACE elsewhere.
findings.rewrite_direction: Target span: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3422-3688. The driving sections between cities (3498-3510, 3553-3560) and the final drive home (3634-3688) are the primary targets for formula reduction. The 'he thought about' inventory at lines 3636-3683 should be cut by at least half — instead of listing every topic Doug processes, pick 2-3 and render them with specificity. At line 3683, the depreciation metaphor ('the damage was structural, not cosmetic') is earned but the sentence that follows ('no amount of rehearsal was going to bring the depreciated value of his performance up to replacement cost') extends the metaphor past its landing — cut the extension. Aim for no more than 5 negation-correction pairs in the full chapter (down from ~12) and no more than 5 'which was' appositives (down from ~11).

findings.acceptance_test: Re-read line 3656 and surrounding context. The phrase 'The material doesn't work' must not appear. Doug's replacement must name the same failure using insurance-adjuster or Doug-specific vocabulary that shows he has processed the three performances into his own diagnosis.
findings.problem: Style-bible example line leakage. Simone's calibration-only line 'The material doesn't work' is echoed verbatim at line 3656, spoken by Doug during the post-Detroit phone call. This is the second occurrence of this calibration phrase in the manuscript (also at line 2112 in Chapter 9).
findings.rewrite_direction: Target: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3656. Replace with Doug's own formulation of the same realization. By Chapter 14, Doug has failed three times — he should have his own vocabulary for this failure by now. Options: he could describe the material as a denied claim, a cosmetic fix over structural damage, or 'the set list is a filing system and nobody wants to watch someone file.' The replacement should show that Doug has internalized the diagnosis through his own language, not through Simone's.

findings.acceptance_test: Re-read the full revised Chapter 14. The chapter must contain no more than two fully rendered performance scenes. If Cleveland is preserved, it must be 15 lines or fewer. The post-show processing must use at least two different formats across the remaining cities (not every one can end with a Simone phone call following the 'How was it? / Bad. / Bad how?' template). The total chapter length should be at least 50 lines shorter than current. The Detroit unscripted 8-minute segment must be preserved in full.
findings.problem: Dramatic recurrence. The three-city tour restages the same insight three times: in Cleveland (scripted material fails, the material-as-costume realization), in Pittsburgh (material fails, 90 seconds of the pool story work accidentally, then the return to material kills it), and in Detroit (material fails, heckler disrupts, 8 minutes of unscripted talk work, then the return to material kills it). The verdict is identical each time: spontaneity works, planning kills it. Cleveland and Pittsburgh deliver the same insight Detroit does, but with less dramatic pressure and no new information. The chapter also repeats a phone-call-after-performance template: each city ends with Doug calling Simone, the calls following the same ladder ('How was it?' / 'Bad.' / 'Bad how?' / specific diagnosis / Simone's brief comment). Cleveland contributes no unique dramatic content that Pittsburgh and Detroit don't deliver better. Pittsburgh's 90-second pool-story moment is valuable but could be folded into the Detroit section.
findings.rewrite_direction: Target span: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3422-3688. Option A (preferred): Cut the Cleveland section entirely (3422-3497, ~75 lines) and open the chapter with Pittsburgh. The Pittsburgh pool-story breakthrough and the Detroit heckler-to-unscripted sequence together deliver the full arc (failure → accidental breakthrough → failure when returning to script). This gives the chapter two distinct beats instead of three identical ones. Option B: Radically compress Cleveland into 10-15 lines of summary (a sentence on the venue, a sentence on the performance, a sentence on the result) and preserve Pittsburgh and Detroit as full scenes. For the phone calls: vary the post-show processing — not every city needs the same Simone call. Detroit's call is the strongest; Pittsburgh could end with Doug's solitary reflection instead.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's greatest risk is timidity. The premise — a man who is not funny trying to be funny — invites a safe, gently comic execution where Doug is lovably bumbling and everything works out. Resist this. Doug's failures should be genuinely uncomfortable to witness. His loneliness should be genuinely lonely, not charmingly solitary. The toast should contain things that are genuinely inappropriate, not safely edgy. The comedy should come from truth that hurts, not from situations that are merely awkward. The moments of tenderness — the Gloria chapter, the Kelsey lunch, the Simone conversation — should be earned through accumulated specificity, not through the narrative signaling that it's time to feel. Every chapter should contain at least one moment that a cautious writer would soften, and the drafting agent should not soften it.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism is a craft failure. If Doug is drunk, describe what drunk looks and feels like — the specific sensory experience, not the label. If Doug is lonely, show the loneliness through the objects and routines that fill the space where other people would be — the cereal dinner, the second bedroom, the unpacked boxes. If a scene involves embarrassment, shame, failure, or longing, render it in full physical and emotional detail. The reader should feel it, not be told about it. Replace every euphemism with the specific thing it is hiding.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for most characters. Uncontracted forms should be reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific moments (Doug going precise under stress, Megan's composed register). If a line reads stiff and the character is not being deliberately formal, contract it.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should share the same structural rhythm. Vary: scene count (1-4), dialogue density (high-dialogue chapters followed by interior-heavy chapters), pacing (frenetic chapters followed by still ones), emotional register (comic escalation followed by tender quiet), narrative distance (very close interior followed by observed social scenes). The novel should feel like it breathes — tight chapters followed by loose ones, crowded chapters followed by solitary ones. Chapter 3 (quiet office day) must feel structurally different from Chapter 10 (quiet visit to Gloria). Chapter 9 (the corporate disaster) must feel structurally different from Chapter 14 (the tour), even though both involve performance failure. Achieve variety through scene construction, not through gimmick.
prose_style_profile.diction: Mid-register, clean, precise. No pyrotechnics. No extended metaphors. The prose should have the quality of someone describing what happened accurately — not artlessly, but without reaching for beauty. When beauty arrives, it should feel accidental, like Doug's humor: a precisely observed detail that turns out to be devastating not because the prose reached for devastation but because the detail was exact. Insurance language may surface in narration when it captures something emotional more precisely than emotional language would. Avoid lyrical flights, extended similes, or any construction that would sound wrong coming from a narrator who is sympathetic to Doug's plainness.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, tightly bound to Doug's perspective. The narrator has access to Doug's interior — his observations, his assessments, his emotional processing through objects and details — but not to any other character's thoughts. Other characters are rendered through Doug's perception: what they say, how they move, what Doug notices about them. The narrator's voice is distinct from Doug's voice but sympathetic to it — slightly more articulate, slightly more aware, but never mocking and never superior. The humor in the narration comes from the same source as Doug's humor: precision about mundane things that reveals the absurdity or sadness underneath. The narrator does not explain the comedy. The narrator does not wink.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and controlled. Default to mid-length sentences with clean syntax. Use short sentences for comic timing — the flat declarative that follows an elaborate setup. Use longer, more syntactically complex sentences for interior passages where Doug is processing something he cannot name. Avoid uniform sentence length across paragraphs. The prose should breathe — alternating between compression and expansion, between observation and reflection, between the external world and Doug's interior. Comedy requires rhythm more than any other mode: the setup needs room, the punch needs precision, and the space after needs silence.
